purpose: Canonical name policy for the Galerina monorepo, so confusingly-similar names cannot recur (RD-0124). Born from the graph-project / project-graph near-anagram tangle. Enforced by scripts/audit-name-collisions.mjs. The package set is the SOURCE OF TRUTH (read live from packages-galerina/*/package.json) — this file holds POLICY (conventions, the collision rule, the known-collision allowlist + decided resolutions, and any extra named entities to also guard).

dirConvention.note: A package's directory name is galerina-<...> and its npm name is @galerina/<...> with the 'galerina-' stripped.

collisionPolicy.explanation: Two package names may NOT share the same token-multiset (the set of '-'/'@'/'/'-split tokens, order-independent). 'devtools-graph-project' and 'devtools-project-graph' have the identical token-multiset {devtools, graph, project} and are a confusing reordered-token collision. Also flags Levenshtein <= 1 typo-twins.
collisionPolicy.severity: error (zero new collisions); KNOWN collisions below are allowlisted while their resolution is pending

knownCollisions.status: ALLOWLISTED — resolution decided, execution pending (see decidedRenames)
knownCollisions.note: graph-project = the CONTRACTS/schema layer (tracked, zero code consumers). project-graph = the ENGINE (data structures + algorithms + reporting; 3 consumers; currently a stray NESTED git clone of a personal repo — gitignored, see .gitignore). The collision is documented + scheduled, so the guard treats it as known, not a new violation.

CPU and GPU are standard, unambiguous compute-target acronyms that happen to differ by one letter. Reviewed and accepted as distinct; allowlisted so the typo-twin detector stays useful for catching ACCIDENTAL near-duplicates without flagging this intended pair.

decidedRenames.reason: It is literally 'project knowledge graph CONTRACTS' (its own package.json description). 'graph-contracts' removes the anagram and is self-describing.
decidedRenames.blastRadius: galerina.workspace.json (3 refs), version.json, core-cli/package.json (build+typecheck scripts), core-cli/src/graph-command.ts (relative-path import), AGENTS.md + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + a few KB docs, regenerated build/graph artifacts, and the package's own dir + .graph.
decidedRenames.blockedOn: a build-verifiable session (the relative-path import + build scripts in core-cli must be updated and the per-package build re-run; not safe to do blind in an autonomous loop).
decidedRenames.executionPlan: git mv the dir; update name in its package.json; update the 4 load-bearing refs (workspace.json, version.json, core-cli build scripts, core-cli graph-command import); regenerate the graph; sweep docs; run core-cli build + typecheck to verify.

decidedRenames.reason: It is 'graph data structures, algorithms, and runtime reporting' = an engine. 'graph-engine' removes the anagram and is self-describing.
decidedRenames.blockedOn: OPERATOR de-clone first — this package is currently a stray nested git clone (its own .git/ + a personal remote). The operator must remove the inner .git/ and commit the source in-tree (it is REAL and depended-upon by 3 packages; do NOT delete) BEFORE it can be renamed. Then update its 3 consumers.

otherGuardedNames.note: Extra named entities to include in the collision check beyond live package names. Empty for v1 — package names are the recurring problem. Diagnostic-code families are guarded separately by scripts/audit-diagnostic-codes.mjs (one code = one name) and scripts/audit-diagnostic-doc-drift.mjs (doc matches source).

crossScopePointers.diagnosticFamilies: FUNGI-<FAMILY>-NNN and ERR_* — canonical list + invariants in scripts/lib/codes.mjs + scripts/audit-diagnostic-codes.mjs; doc taxonomy in docs/Knowledge-Bases/compiler-diagnostics.md (kept honest by audit-diagnostic-doc-drift.mjs).
crossScopePointers.rdSeries: RD-NNNN — R&D corpus in the Galerina-R-AND-D repo; KB write-ups as docs/Knowledge-Bases/galerina-rd-NNNN-*.md. RD-id uniqueness is owner-managed (future: add to otherGuardedNames to machine-enforce).
